facet: tc netem traffic shaping — do the tiered delivery guarantees hold or degrade VISIBLY (never silently) under added latency/loss?
topology: relay + responder on node-1 (ens5, 172.31.43.122); generator (relay-only passthrough, 12 conns x 256KiB, concurrency 6) on node-2 (172.31.19.13). tc applied ONLY to node-1->node-2 egress via prio qdisc + u32 dst filter, so the Mac's SSH path is unshaped and a watchdog clears tc after 240s regardless.
method: For each condition (baseline / +100ms delay / 10% loss / 30% loss) set the netem qdisc on the filtered band, run the generator, parse the relay-path RTT (p50/mean/max) + established/failed + establish_ms from the generator JSON summary.

result.exit: MET — under every shaping condition all 12 connections still ESTABLISHED (0 failed): the eventual-delivery guarantee holds. Degradation is VISIBLE in the client-measurable RTT and establish time (delay100: RTT p50 25->250ms; loss30: establish 5.6s->28.4s, RTT max ->946ms), never a silent drop. QUIC degrades gracefully (heavier retransmit) rather than thrashing/collapsing.
result.ti4_mapping: Interactive tier (prompt + fail-signal): the added latency is measurable client-side via conn.paths() RTT, so a UI can surface 'slow/degraded' rather than hang silently. Eventual tier: delivery completed under all conditions incl. 30% loss. Best-effort: never needed to drop — nothing silently lost.
result.caveat: Egress shaping only (node-1->node-2); ingress is the app-layer client_rx limiter, not exercised here. Loss applied to the relay->client leg; the client->relay leg unshaped (asymmetric). Bandwidth-cap (rate) condition not run — delay+loss covered the visible-degradation claim. No sustained-throughput-under-shaping bulk test; this measures connection establishment + control-plane RTT, not steady-state goodput under netem.
